title: 1.1: Identifies that DocumentClassifier uses SHA1 hash comparison (self.data_hash vs new_data_hash) in the train() method as the retraining decision mechanism
1.2: Reports that predict_correspondent() returns the highest probability class without any minimum confidence threshold as the prediction acceptance behavior
1.4: Identifies that RasterisedDocumentParser invokes OCR via ocrmypdf.ocr() Python API as the OCR invocation method
1.6: Identifies that DirectoriesMixin setUp() calls setup_directories() to create isolated temporary directories for test isolation
1.7: Reports that training occurs before document consumption in the test setup when train_classifier() is called for training timing
1.8: Lists OCR parameters passed to ocrmypdf: language, output_type, use_threads, jobs, progress_bar, and skip_text/force_ocr based on OCR_MODE for the OCR configuration
1.9: Reports that mime type is detected via magic.from_file(path, mime=True) before parsing as the mime type detection method

1.11: Identifies that pytest.mark.skip decorator is ignored by Django's unittest test runner, causing test_load_classifier_cached to execute unexpectedly as a source of non-deterministic test behavior

1.12: Reports that 2 documents are created from a PDF with one separator barcode (patch-code-t-middle.pdf) and 3 documents from a PDF with two separator barcodes (several-patcht-codes.pdf) for barcode splitting behavior
1.13: Identifies 'PATCHT' as the default barcode string value from CONSUMER_BARCODE_STRING setting that triggers document splitting
1.15: Explains that barcode splitting affects classifier training data by creating multiple Document records from a single input file, each consumed separately with its own OCR-extracted content
2.1: Claims that async_task() executes tasks synchronously in production for background task behavior
